articles.headline: National Green Hydrogen Mission: India Unveils Phase II for Accelerated Deployment
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet on National Sports Day approved the launch of Phase II of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, focusing on scaling up production and establishing export capabilities.
• This phase aims to incentivize manufacturing of electrolysers and develop green hydrogen hubs across key industrial corridors.
• Emphasis will be placed on R&D for cost reduction, skill development, and creating a robust regulatory framework.
• The mission seeks to position India as a global leader in green hydrogen production and utilization, contributing significantly to decarbonization targets.
• Public-private partnerships and international collaborations are key components of the strategy to attract investment and technology.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Investment models
Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation, environmental impact assessment
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission launched in 2023.
Phase II focuses on scaling up production and export capabilities.
Key components include electrolyser manufacturing incentives and green hydrogen hubs.
Aims for 5 MMT of green hydrogen production by 2030.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's energy security and climate change commitments through green hydrogen.
Analyze the economic potential and challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production.
Evaluate the role of government policies like PLI schemes in fostering a domestic green hydrogen ecosystem.
Examine the geopolitical implications of India becoming a green hydrogen exporter.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen production facility with solar panels and wind turbines in the background.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all normal matter.

articles.headline: Renowned Konkani Music Composer Eric Ozario Passes Away at 76
articles.explainer:
• Eric Ozario, a towering figure in Konkani music and culture, passed away at the age of 76.
• Known for his soulful compositions and efforts to promote Konkani language and traditions, he was a recipient of numerous awards.
• Ozario was instrumental in establishing Mandd Sobhann, a pioneering organization dedicated to preserving and promoting Konkani culture globally.
• His work encompassed over four decades, creating a rich legacy of songs, stage shows, and cultural festivals.
• His demise marks a significant loss for the Konkani community and Indian regional arts.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Salient features of Indian Society
prelimsPoints: Eric Ozario was a prominent Konkani music composer and cultural activist.
He was associated with Mandd Sobhann, an organization for Konkani culture.
Konkani is an Indo-Aryan language spoken primarily in Goa, Karnataka, and Maharashtra.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of cultural organizations like Mandd Sobhann in preserving regional languages and art forms.
Analyze the challenges faced by regional languages and cultures in the modern era and potential solutions.
Examine the contribution of individuals like Eric Ozario to India's diverse cultural tapestry.
articles.imageDescription: Portrait of Eric Ozario, a smiling man, perhaps holding a musical instrument.
articles.trivia: Konkani is one of the 22 scheduled languages of India and is written in multiple scripts, including Devanagari (official), Roman, Kannada, Malayalam, and Arabic.

articles.headline: National Sports Day: Government Unveils 'Khelo India Vision 2047' for Sporting Excellence
articles.explainer:
• On the occasion of National Sports Day, commemorating Major Dhyan Chand's birth anniversary, the Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports launched 'Khelo India Vision 2047'.
• The long-term vision aims to develop India into a global sporting powerhouse by 2047, coinciding with 100 years of independence.
• Key pillars include grassroots talent identification, world-class infrastructure development, enhanced coach education, and robust sports science support.
• The initiative also focuses on promoting sports culture in schools and colleges and ensuring equitable access for all sections of society.
• Special emphasis will be given to indigenous sports and para-sports development.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of Indian Society
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
articles.source: Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports
prelimsPoints: National Sports Day is celebrated on August 29th, Major Dhyan Chand's birth anniversary.
Khelo India program aims to revive sports culture in India.
'Khelo India Vision 2047' is a long-term plan for sporting excellence.
Focus on grassroots, infrastructure, coach education, and sports science.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of sports in national development and youth empowerment.
Analyze the effectiveness of government initiatives like Khelo India in promoting sports at various levels.
Suggest measures to overcome challenges in developing a robust sports ecosystem in India.
Evaluate the potential impact of 'Khelo India Vision 2047' on India's global sporting presence.
articles.imageDescription: A collage showing young athletes practicing various sports under the Khelo India banner.
articles.trivia: Major Dhyan Chand, known as 'The Wizard' or 'The Magician' of hockey, scored over 400 goals in his international career.

articles.headline: RBI's Monetary Policy Review: Inflationary Pressures Keep Rates Steady Amidst Global Volatility
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) concluded its Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting, maintaining the repo rate at its current level.
• The decision reflects persistent concerns over inflation, primarily driven by volatile food prices and global commodity fluctuations.
• RBI reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the target while supporting economic growth.
• Experts anticipate a cautious approach by the central bank, closely monitoring both domestic and international economic indicators.
• Liquidity management measures were also discussed to ensure financial stability.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) sets the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India.
RBI's primary objective is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
CPI is the key inflation indicator in India.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth.
Discuss the effectiveness of various monetary policy tools in managing liquidity and inflation.
Examine the impact of global economic uncertainties on India's monetary policy decisions.
Evaluate the role of the MPC in ensuring transparency and accountability in monetary policy formulation.
articles.imageDescription: RBI logo with graphs showing economic trends in the background.
articles.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, in accordance with the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Reaffirms Digital Privacy as Fundamental Right, Sets Guidelines for Data Protection
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a landmark judgment, reiterating that the right to digital privacy is an intrinsic part of the fundamental right to life and personal liberty under Article 21.
• The ruling came in response to a batch of petitions concerning data sharing practices of private entities and government surveillance.
• The court laid down stringent guidelines for data collection, storage, and processing, emphasizing the need for informed consent and data minimization.
• It directed the government to expedite the enactment of a comprehensive data protection law that aligns with global best practices.
• This judgment is expected to have far-reaching implications for technology companies, government agencies, and individual users.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Right to Privacy was declared a fundamental right by the Supreme Court in the Puttaswamy judgment (2017).
Article 21 of the Indian Constitution deals with the protection of life and personal liberty.
Data protection law (DPDP Act 2023) is already in place, but new guidelines could refine its interpretation/implementation.
The judgment emphasizes informed consent and data minimization principles.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution of the right to privacy in India and its significance in the digital age.
Analyze the challenges in balancing national security, economic growth, and individual privacy rights.
Evaluate the adequacy of existing data protection frameworks in India and suggest improvements.
Examine the impact of such judicial pronouncements on government policies and corporate practices regarding data.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel resting on a stack of law books, with binary code flowing in the background.
articles.trivia: The landmark K.S. Puttaswamy v. Union of India case in 2017 formally recognized the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right.

articles.headline: India-ASEAN Summit Concludes: Pledges for Enhanced Trade, Digital Connectivity, and Maritime Security
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Summit concluded with leaders reaffirming their commitment to strengthening the Comprehensive Strategic Partnership.
• Key outcomes include agreements to boost bilateral trade to $200 billion by 2030 and enhance supply chain resilience.
• Discussions focused on expanding digital connectivity, promoting innovation, and cooperation in emerging technologies.
• Leaders also addressed regional security challenges, particularly in the Indo-Pacific, and emphasized maritime cooperation.
• India reiterated its 'Act East' policy and its vision for a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: ASEAN is a regional intergovernmental organization of ten Southeast Asian countries.
India's 'Act East' policy is a successor to the 'Look East' policy.
India and ASEAN elevated their relationship to a Comprehensive Strategic Partnership in 2022.
Key areas of cooperation include trade, connectivity, and maritime security.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of India-ASEAN relations for India's strategic and economic interests.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in deepening India's engagement with Southeast Asia.
Evaluate the role of India and ASEAN in maintaining stability and prosperity in the Indo-Pacific region.
Examine the impact of global geopolitical shifts on India's 'Act East' policy.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and ASEAN member states displayed together during a summit.
articles.trivia: ASEAN was established on August 8, 1967, in Bangkok, Thailand, with five founding members: Indonesia, Malaysia, Philippines, Singapore, and Thailand.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Critical Test of Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Landing Technology
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieved a significant milestone by successfully conducting a crucial test of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) landing technology.
• The test involved a scaled-down RLV prototype performing an autonomous landing on a designated runway, simulating conditions for future orbital re-entry.
• This demonstration is vital for developing India's capabilities in cost-effective space access and sustainable space exploration.
• The RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) project aims to develop all the necessary technologies for a Two Stage To Orbit (TSTO) fully reusable launch vehicle.
• Future phases will focus on orbital flight and re-entry experiments.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space, Computers, Robotics, Nano-technology, Bio-technology and issues relating to Intellectual Property Rights.
prelimsPoints: ISRO's RLV-TD project aims to develop reusable launch vehicle technology.
The test demonstrated autonomous landing capabilities.
Reusable launch vehicles reduce the cost of space missions.
India is among a few countries developing RLV technology.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of reusable launch vehicle technology for India's space program.
Analyze how RLV technology can make space access more affordable and sustainable.
Examine the potential applications and economic benefits of advanced space transportation systems.
Compare India's RLV efforts with those of other spacefaring nations.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of ISRO's RLV-TD descending towards a runway.
articles.trivia: The first RLV-TD 'HEX' (Hypersonic Flight Experiment) was successfully tested by ISRO in 2016.

articles.headline: New Report Highlights Accelerated Melting of Himalayan Glaciers, Threatening Regional Water Security
articles.explainer:
• A comprehensive report released by a consortium of environmental agencies revealed an alarming rate of melting of Himalayan glaciers.
• The study, based on satellite imagery and ground observations, indicates that glaciers are receding faster than previously estimated, posing a severe threat to water resources in South Asia.
• The accelerated melting is attributed to rising global temperatures and localized black carbon deposition.
• Consequences include increased flood risks in the short term and severe water scarcity for millions in the long term.
• The report calls for urgent international cooperation and stricter climate mitigation policies to protect this critical freshwater source.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
prelimsPoints: Himalayan glaciers are a critical source of water for major Asian rivers.
Glacier melting is accelerated by rising global temperatures and black carbon.
Impacts include increased flood risks and long-term water scarcity.
The Himalayas are often referred to as the 'Third Pole'.
mainsPoints: Analyze the multifaceted impacts of Himalayan glacier retreat on India and other South Asian countries.
Discuss the policy measures and international collaborations required to address the issue of climate change and glacier melting.
Examine the role of sustainable development practices and disaster management strategies in mitigating the consequences.
Evaluate the socio-economic implications of water scarcity due to glacier melt on agriculture and livelihoods.
articles.imageDescription: A stark image of a receding Himalayan glacier with exposed rock and meltwater.
articles.trivia: The Himalayas contain the third-largest deposit of ice and snow in the world, after the Arctic and Antarctic, earning them the nickname 'The Third Pole'.

articles.headline: Government Accelerates Border Infrastructure Development to Enhance Connectivity and Vigilance
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence announced a significant push for upgrading border infrastructure, including roads, bridges, and advanced surveillance systems.
• The initiative aims to improve troop mobility, facilitate trade with neighboring countries, and enhance overall border security.
• Special focus is on strategically important regions along the northern and eastern borders, often characterized by challenging terrain.
• The project utilizes advanced engineering techniques and incorporates environmental sustainability measures.
• This development is crucial for national security and regional economic integration, bolstering India's defensive and logistical capabilities.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Border Roads Organization (BRO) is responsible for building and maintaining border roads.
Infrastructure development is crucial for troop movement and logistical support.
Focus on northern and eastern borders, including the LAC.
Enhances both defensive capabilities and regional trade.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of robust border infrastructure for India's national security.
Analyze the challenges involved in developing infrastructure in remote and difficult border areas.
Examine the role of border infrastructure in promoting regional trade and economic development.
Evaluate the impact of technological advancements in surveillance on border management.
articles.imageDescription: A newly constructed bridge traversing a mountainous terrain in a border region.
articles.trivia: The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) was formed in 1960 and has constructed over 60,000 km of roads in India's border areas.

articles.headline: 'Poshan Abhiyaan' Progress Report: Significant Strides in Tackling Malnutrition, Challenges Remain
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development released its annual progress report on 'Poshan Abhiyaan' (National Nutrition Mission).
• The report highlighted significant reductions in stunting, wasting, and anemia among children and women across several states.
• Key successes were attributed to improved convergence of various schemes, community mobilization, and technological interventions like the Poshan Tracker.
• However, challenges persist, particularly in remote areas and among marginalized communities, requiring intensified efforts.
• The mission aims to achieve a malnutrition-free India by 2030, emphasizing a life-cycle approach to nutrition.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
articles.source: Ministry of Women and Child Development
prelimsPoints: Poshan Abhiyaan (National Nutrition Mission) launched in 2018.
Aims to reduce stunting, wasting, anemia, and low birth weight.
Implemented by Ministry of Women and Child Development.
Utilizes technology like Poshan Tracker for monitoring.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-sectoral approach adopted by Poshan Abhiyaan to address malnutrition in India.
Analyze the socio-economic factors contributing to malnutrition and the challenges in its eradication.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government welfare schemes in improving nutritional outcomes.
Suggest policy interventions and community-level strategies to achieve a malnutrition-free India.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic illustrating various components of Poshan Abhiyaan, with children and women at the center.
articles.trivia: The word 'Poshan' means nutrition in Hindi. The mission was launched from Jhunjhunu, Rajasthan.

articles.headline: PLI Scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) Battery Manufacturing Gains Momentum
articles.explainer:
• The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) battery manufacturing is showing robust progress, attracting significant investments.
• Several domestic and international players have committed to setting up large-scale battery manufacturing facilities in India.
• This initiative is crucial for India's push towards electric mobility and renewable energy storage, reducing reliance on imports.
• The scheme offers incentives based on sales of manufactured goods, promoting 'Make in India' and creating a domestic supply chain.
• It is expected to generate substantial employment and reduce the carbon footprint of the transportation and energy sectors.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: PLI scheme for ACC batteries aims to boost domestic manufacturing.
ACC batteries are critical for electric vehicles and renewable energy storage.
The scheme is administered by the Ministry of Heavy Industries.
It promotes 'Make in India' and reduces import dependence.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of PLI schemes in boosting India's manufacturing sector and achieving self-reliance.
Analyze the importance of ACC battery manufacturing for India's electric vehicle and renewable energy targets.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in establishing a robust domestic battery manufacturing ecosystem.
Evaluate the economic and environmental benefits of localizing ACC battery production.
articles.imageDescription: An illustration of an electric car battery pack with the 'Make in India' logo.
articles.trivia: The PLI scheme for ACC battery manufacturing has an outlay of ₹18,100 crore over five years.

articles.headline: Delimitation Commission for North-Eastern States: Progress and Challenges in Electoral Reorganization
articles.explainer:
• The Delimitation Commission, tasked with redrawing Lok Sabha and Assembly constituencies in certain North-Eastern states, released its latest progress report.
• The exercise aims to ensure equitable representation based on population and geographical contiguity, as mandated by the Constitution.
• Challenges include addressing concerns of tribal communities, preserving administrative units, and managing local political sensitivities.
• The process is crucial for fair electoral representation and strengthening democratic institutions in the region.
• Public consultations are underway to gather feedback from various stakeholders before finalizing the proposals.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
prelimsPoints: Delimitation is the act of redrawing boundaries of Lok Sabha and state assembly constituencies.
Delimitation Commission is appointed by the President of India and works in collaboration with the Election Commission.
Its orders have the force of law and cannot be challenged in any court.
Last nationwide delimitation was completed in 2002, based on 2001 census data, but seats frozen until 2026.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional provisions and objectives behind the delimitation exercise in India.
Analyze the unique challenges faced by the Delimitation Commission in the North-Eastern states, particularly concerning tribal representation.
Examine the impact of delimitation on political representation, regional balance, and democratic fairness.
Evaluate the role of public consultations in ensuring transparency and addressing local concerns during the delimitation process.
articles.imageDescription: A map of North-Eastern states with overlaid constituency boundaries.
articles.trivia: The first Delimitation Commission was constituted in 1952. The next delimitation for the entire country is expected after 2026.

articles.headline: Wetlands Conservation Efforts Intensify: Focus on Ramsar Sites Expansion and Ecological Restoration
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change announced intensified efforts for wetlands conservation, including identifying new potential Ramsar Sites.
• The initiative aims to protect critical wetland ecosystems that provide vital services like water purification, flood control, and biodiversity support.
• Emphasis is placed on community participation, sustainable management practices, and ecological restoration of degraded wetlands.
• India, a signatory to the Ramsar Convention, has significantly expanded its network of 'Wetlands of International Importance' in recent years.
• This push aligns with India's commitments to international biodiversity conventions and sustainable development goals.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and its conservation.
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Ramsar Convention is an international treaty for the conservation and sustainable use of wetlands.
Ramsar Sites are wetlands designated to be of international importance.
India has a significant number of Ramsar Sites (currently 80, but could increase by 2025).
Wetlands provide crucial ecosystem services like water regulation and biodiversity support.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological and economic importance of wetlands and the threats they face in India.
Analyze the role of the Ramsar Convention and national policies in wetlands conservation.
Examine the challenges in managing and restoring wetlands, including encroachment and pollution.
Suggest strategies for effective community-based wetland management and sustainable utilization.
articles.imageDescription: A serene wetland landscape with migratory birds and lush vegetation.
articles.trivia: Chilika Lake in Odisha was the first Indian wetland designated as a Ramsar Site in 1981.

articles.headline: Remembering Major Dhyan Chand: A Legacy of Sporting Excellence and National Pride on His Birth Anniversary
articles.explainer:
• National Sports Day, celebrated on August 29th, marks the birth anniversary of the legendary hockey player Major Dhyan Chand.
• Dhyan Chand's unparalleled skill and leadership led India to three consecutive Olympic gold medals in hockey (1928, 1932, 1936).
• His legacy continues to inspire generations of athletes and symbolizes the golden era of Indian hockey.
• The day serves as a reminder of the importance of sports in national character building, physical fitness, and promoting a culture of excellence.
• Various awards, including the Dhyan Chand Award for Lifetime Achievement in Sports and Games, are presented on this day.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Modern Indian history from about the middle of the eighteenth century until the present- significant events, personalities, issues.
prelimsPoints: National Sports Day is celebrated on August 29.
It commemorates the birth anniversary of Major Dhyan Chand.
Dhyan Chand led India to three Olympic hockey gold medals (1928, 1932, 1936).
Dhyan Chand Award is India's highest award for lifetime achievement in sports.
mainsPoints: Discuss the historical significance of Major Dhyan Chand's contributions to Indian sports and national identity.
Analyze how sporting achievements can foster national unity and pride.
Examine the evolution of Indian hockey and the challenges it faces in regaining its past glory.
Evaluate the role of National Sports Day in promoting sports culture and encouraging participation at all levels.
articles.imageDescription: A black and white photograph of Major Dhyan Chand playing hockey.
articles.trivia: Adolf Hitler was reportedly so impressed with Dhyan Chand's performance at the 1936 Berlin Olympics that he offered him German citizenship and a higher rank in the German army, which Dhyan Chand declined.

articles.headline: India's First Dedicated Quantum Computing Lab Inaugurated, Boosting Research and Innovation
articles.explainer:
• India's first dedicated Quantum Computing Lab was officially inaugurated, marking a significant leap in the country's technological capabilities.
• The state-of-the-art facility will serve as a hub for advanced research, development, and training in quantum technologies.
• It aims to foster collaboration between academia, industry, and government agencies to accelerate India's position in the global quantum race.
• Initial focus areas include quantum algorithms, quantum cryptography, and quantum sensing, with applications in defense, finance, and healthcare.
• This initiative is a crucial step under the National Quantum Mission, positioning India as a key player in future computing paradigms.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space, Computers, Robotics, Nano-technology, Bio-technology and issues relating to Intellectual Property Rights.
prelimsPoints: Quantum computing utilizes principles of quantum mechanics.
India launched the National Quantum Mission in 2023.
Qubits are the basic units of information in quantum computing.
Applications include drug discovery, cryptography, and AI.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of quantum computing and its strategic importance for India.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in developing a robust quantum technology ecosystem in India.
Examine the ethical and security implications of quantum computing advancements.
Evaluate the role of government missions and private sector investment in accelerating quantum research and development.
articles.imageDescription: An abstract representation of quantum computing, with glowing circuits and intertwined particles.
articles.trivia: Unlike classical computers that use bits (0 or 1), quantum computers use 'qubits' which can represent 0, 1, or both simultaneously through superposition.

articles.headline: PM Jan Dhan Yojana Marks 11th Anniversary: Financial Inclusion Reaches New Milestones
articles.explainer:
• The Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana (PMJDY) completed 11 successful years since its launch, celebrating significant achievements in financial inclusion.
• The scheme has enabled over 50 crore bank accounts, bringing a vast unbanked population into the formal financial system.
• PMJDY accounts have been instrumental in direct benefit transfers (DBT) of various government welfare schemes.
• The initiative has also boosted financial literacy, access to credit, insurance, and pension facilities, especially for women and rural populations.
• Future focus includes deepening digital transactions and promoting micro-insurance products through the Jan Dhan ecosystem.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
prelimsPoints: PMJDY was launched on August 28, 2014, for financial inclusion.
Provides basic savings bank accounts, RuPay debit cards, and overdraft facilities.
Key for Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) of government welfare schemes.
Administered by the Ministry of Finance.
mainsPoints: Discuss the impact of PM Jan Dhan Yojana on financial inclusion and poverty reduction in India.
Analyze how PMJDY has facilitated the effective delivery of government welfare schemes through DBT.
Examine the challenges in achieving complete financial literacy and digital penetration among PMJDY account holders.
Evaluate the future potential of the Jan Dhan ecosystem in driving inclusive growth and economic empowerment.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized graphic showing the PMJDY logo with hands holding a RuPay card and mobile phone.
articles.trivia: PMJDY accounts come with a RuPay Debit Card, which also provides accidental insurance cover.

articles.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Emphasizes Enhanced Cooperation in Indo-Pacific and Critical Technologies
articles.explainer:
• The India-Japan Strategic Dialogue concluded with both nations reiterating their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
• Discussions focused on deepening defense and security cooperation, including joint exercises and technology transfers.
• Significant progress was made on collaboration in critical and emerging technologies, such as semiconductors, 5G, and artificial intelligence.
• Both sides also pledged to boost economic ties, infrastructure development, and clean energy projects.
• The dialogue underscored the importance of the Special Strategic and Global Partnership in addressing regional and global challenges.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India and Japan share a 'Special Strategic and Global Partnership'.
Cooperation extends to defense, economy, and critical technologies.
Both nations are part of the QUAD grouping.
Indo-Pacific vision is a key convergence area.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of India-Japan relations in the context of the evolving Indo-Pacific geopolitical landscape.
Discuss the various pillars of cooperation between India and Japan, including defense, economy, and technology.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in enhancing bilateral trade and investment between the two countries.
Evaluate the role of India-Japan partnership in promoting regional stability and economic prosperity.
articles.imageDescription: National flags of India and Japan side-by-side, symbolizing bilateral relations.
articles.trivia: Japan is one of the largest investors in India, particularly in infrastructure projects like the Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail.

articles.headline: G20 Finance Ministers' Meeting Concludes: Focus on Global Economic Stability and Debt Sustainability
articles.explainer:
• The G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors (FMCBG) meeting concluded with a joint communiqué addressing global economic challenges.
• Key discussions revolved around strengthening global financial safety nets, promoting debt sustainability for low-income countries, and coordinating macroeconomic policies.
• Ministers emphasized the need for structural reforms to boost long-term growth and resilience against future shocks.
• Climate finance and digital taxation frameworks were also high on the agenda, seeking equitable solutions.
• The meeting underscored the G20's role in fostering international economic cooperation amidst persistent geopolitical uncertainties.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: G20 is an international forum for governments and central bank governors from 19 countries and the European Union.
Focuses on global economic cooperation.
India held the G20 presidency in 2023.
Key agenda items often include financial stability, debt, and climate change.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the G20 forum in addressing contemporary global economic challenges.
Analyze the role of India in shaping the G20 agenda and promoting the interests of developing countries.
Examine the complexities of achieving global economic stability and debt sustainability in a fragmented world.
Evaluate the progress and challenges in international cooperation on climate finance and digital taxation within the G20 framework.
articles.imageDescription: Official logo of the G20 with flags of member countries in the background.
articles.trivia: The G20 was formed in 1999 in response to the Asian financial crisis as a forum for finance ministers and central bank governors. It was elevated to head of state/government level in 2008.

highlights: National Green Hydrogen Mission Phase II Launched: India aims for accelerated production and export capabilities.
Renowned Konkani music composer Eric Ozario passes away at 76, leaving a rich cultural legacy.
National Sports Day: Government unveils 'Khelo India Vision 2047' for India to become a global sporting powerhouse.
Supreme Court reaffirms digital privacy as a fundamental right, setting new guidelines for data protection.
ISRO successfully conducts critical test of Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) landing technology, boosting cost-effective space access.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Green Hydrogen Mission, consider the following statements:
1. The mission aims to make India a global hub for the production and export of Green Hydrogen.
2. It primarily focuses on the development of hydrogen fuel cell vehicles.
3. The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for electrolyser manufacturing is a key component of the mission.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export. Statement 2 is incorrect. While hydrogen fuel cell vehicles are an application, the mission's primary focus is broader, encompassing production, utilization in various sectors (refineries, fertilizer, steel, shipping, transport), and R&D for the entire value chain, not just fuel cell vehicles. Statement 3 is correct. A PLI scheme for manufacturing electrolysers and production of green hydrogen is a key component to incentivize domestic industry.

prelimsMocks.question: On which date is National Sports Day celebrated in India, commemorating the birth anniversary of Major Dhyan Chand?
prelimsMocks.explanation: National Sports Day in India is celebrated on August 29th each year, which is the birth anniversary of the legendary Indian hockey player, Major Dhyan Chand.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a primary objective of the Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana (PMJDY)?
options: (a) Providing universal access to banking facilities.
(b) Facilitating Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) of government schemes.
(d) Providing subsidized loans for agricultural machinery.
prelimsMocks.answer: (d) Providing subsidized loans for agricultural machinery.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The primary objectives of PMJDY include universal access to banking facilities, facilitating DBT, promoting financial literacy, and digital transactions. While access to credit is part of financial inclusion, providing subsidized loans specifically for agricultural machinery is not a direct primary objective of PMJDY itself, but rather falls under other specialized agricultural schemes.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Ramsar Convention:
1. It is an international treaty for the conservation and sustainable use of wetlands.
2. Chilika Lake was the first site in India to be designated as a Ramsar Site.
3. All wetlands in India are automatically designated as Ramsar Sites upon notification by the state government.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. The Ramsar Convention is indeed an international treaty for wetlands conservation. Statement 2 is correct. Chilika Lake in Odisha was among the first two sites (along with Keoladeo National Park) in India designated as a Ramsar Site in 1981. Statement 3 is incorrect. Wetlands are designated as Ramsar Sites based on specific criteria and a nomination process, not automatically.

prelimsMocks.question: The term 'qubit' is associated with which of the following fields of technology?
prelimsMocks.explanation: 'Qubit' (quantum bit) is the basic unit of information in quantum computing, analogous to the bit in classical computing. Unlike bits, qubits can exist in superposition, representing 0, 1, or both simultaneously.

mainsMocks.question: The launch of Phase II of the National Green Hydrogen Mission signifies India's commitment to energy transition and climate goals. Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and its potential to transform key industrial sectors. (150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Infrastructure: Energy; Environmental pollution and degradation; Conservation
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce Green Hydrogen Mission and its goals. Explain strategic importance for energy security (reduced fossil fuel imports). Discuss transformation potential in sectors like refining, fertilizers, steel, and transport. Conclude with environmental benefits and global leadership ambition.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's reaffirmation of digital privacy as a fundamental right underscores the evolving challenges in the digital age. Analyze the implications of this judgment for individual liberties, government surveillance, and the responsibilities of technology companies in India. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—fundamental rights; Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin with the context of the judgment and Article 21. Discuss implications for individual liberties (autonomy, control over personal data). Address government surveillance (need for proportionality, legality). Examine responsibilities of tech companies (data minimization, consent, transparency). Conclude with the need for a robust data protection framework and balancing competing interests.

mainsMocks.question: On National Sports Day, India reiterates its vision for sporting excellence. Evaluate the effectiveness of government initiatives like Khelo India in fostering a robust sports culture and identifying grassroots talent. What more needs to be done to make India a global sporting powerhouse? (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS1: Salient features of Indian Society; Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce National Sports Day and the 'Khelo India Vision 2047'. Evaluate Khelo India's successes (talent identification, infrastructure, sports culture promotion) and limitations. Suggest additional measures: enhanced sports science, private sector involvement, school-level sports, better coach development, financial support for athletes, and focus on indigenous games and para-sports.

quickBytes.text: August 29 is National Sports Day in India, celebrating the birth anniversary of Major Dhyan Chand.
The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims for India to become a global hub for green hydrogen production.
The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is the central bank of India, established in 1935.
Article 21 of the Indian Constitution guarantees the protection of life and personal liberty.
ASEAN is a regional intergovernmental organization of ten Southeast Asian countries.
ISRO's RLV-TD project aims to develop Reusable Launch Vehicle technology for cost-effective space access.
The Himalayas are often referred to as the 'Third Pole' due to their vast ice and snow reserves.
Poshan Abhiyaan (National Nutrition Mission) aims to reduce malnutrition among children and women.
The G20 is an international forum for cooperation on matters of global economy and finance.
Quantum computing uses 'qubits' which can exist in superposition, unlike classical bits.

GS1.headline: Renowned Konkani Music Composer Eric Ozario Passes Away at 76
GS1.explainer:
• Eric Ozario, a towering figure in Konkani music and culture, passed away at the age of 76.
• Known for his soulful compositions and efforts to promote Konkani language and traditions, he was a recipient of numerous awards.
• Ozario was instrumental in establishing Mandd Sobhann, a pioneering organization dedicated to preserving and promoting Konkani culture globally.
• His work encompassed over four decades, creating a rich legacy of songs, stage shows, and cultural festivals.
• His demise marks a significant loss for the Konkani community and Indian regional arts.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Salient features of Indian Society
prelimsPoints: Eric Ozario was a prominent Konkani music composer and cultural activist.
He was associated with Mandd Sobhann, an organization for Konkani culture.
Konkani is an Indo-Aryan language spoken primarily in Goa, Karnataka, and Maharashtra.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of cultural organizations like Mandd Sobhann in preserving regional languages and art forms.
Analyze the challenges faced by regional languages and cultures in the modern era and potential solutions.
Examine the contribution of individuals like Eric Ozario to India's diverse cultural tapestry.
GS1.imageDescription: Portrait of Eric Ozario, a smiling man, perhaps holding a musical instrument.
GS1.trivia: Konkani is one of the 22 scheduled languages of India and is written in multiple scripts, including Devanagari (official), Roman, Kannada, Malayalam, and Arabic.

GS1.headline: National Sports Day: Government Unveils 'Khelo India Vision 2047' for Sporting Excellence
GS1.explainer:
• On the occasion of National Sports Day, commemorating Major Dhyan Chand's birth anniversary, the Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports launched 'Khelo India Vision 2047'.
• The long-term vision aims to develop India into a global sporting powerhouse by 2047, coinciding with 100 years of independence.
• Key pillars include grassroots talent identification, world-class infrastructure development, enhanced coach education, and robust sports science support.
• The initiative also focuses on promoting sports culture in schools and colleges and ensuring equitable access for all sections of society.
• Special emphasis will be given to indigenous sports and para-sports development.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of Indian Society
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
GS1.source: Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports
prelimsPoints: National Sports Day is celebrated on August 29th, Major Dhyan Chand's birth anniversary.
Khelo India program aims to revive sports culture in India.
'Khelo India Vision 2047' is a long-term plan for sporting excellence.
Focus on grassroots, infrastructure, coach education, and sports science.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of sports in national development and youth empowerment.
Analyze the effectiveness of government initiatives like Khelo India in promoting sports at various levels.
Suggest measures to overcome challenges in developing a robust sports ecosystem in India.
Evaluate the potential impact of 'Khelo India Vision 2047' on India's global sporting presence.
GS1.imageDescription: A collage showing young athletes practicing various sports under the Khelo India banner.
GS1.trivia: Major Dhyan Chand, known as 'The Wizard' or 'The Magician' of hockey, scored over 400 goals in his international career.

GS1.headline: Remembering Major Dhyan Chand: A Legacy of Sporting Excellence and National Pride on His Birth Anniversary
GS1.explainer:
• National Sports Day, celebrated on August 29th, marks the birth anniversary of the legendary hockey player Major Dhyan Chand.
• Dhyan Chand's unparalleled skill and leadership led India to three consecutive Olympic gold medals in hockey (1928, 1932, 1936).
• His legacy continues to inspire generations of athletes and symbolizes the golden era of Indian hockey.
• The day serves as a reminder of the importance of sports in national character building, physical fitness, and promoting a culture of excellence.
• Various awards, including the Dhyan Chand Award for Lifetime Achievement in Sports and Games, are presented on this day.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Modern Indian history from about the middle of the eighteenth century until the present- significant events, personalities, issues.
prelimsPoints: National Sports Day is celebrated on August 29.
It commemorates the birth anniversary of Major Dhyan Chand.
Dhyan Chand led India to three Olympic hockey gold medals (1928, 1932, 1936).
Dhyan Chand Award is India's highest award for lifetime achievement in sports.
mainsPoints: Discuss the historical significance of Major Dhyan Chand's contributions to Indian sports and national identity.
Analyze how sporting achievements can foster national unity and pride.
Examine the evolution of Indian hockey and the challenges it faces in regaining its past glory.
Evaluate the role of National Sports Day in promoting sports culture and encouraging participation at all levels.
GS1.imageDescription: A black and white photograph of Major Dhyan Chand playing hockey.
GS1.trivia: Adolf Hitler was reportedly so impressed with Dhyan Chand's performance at the 1936 Berlin Olympics that he offered him German citizenship and a higher rank in the German army, which Dhyan Chand declined.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Reaffirms Digital Privacy as Fundamental Right, Sets Guidelines for Data Protection
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a landmark judgment, reiterating that the right to digital privacy is an intrinsic part of the fundamental right to life and personal liberty under Article 21.
• The ruling came in response to a batch of petitions concerning data sharing practices of private entities and government surveillance.
• The court laid down stringent guidelines for data collection, storage, and processing, emphasizing the need for informed consent and data minimization.
• It directed the government to expedite the enactment of a comprehensive data protection law that aligns with global best practices.
• This judgment is expected to have far-reaching implications for technology companies, government agencies, and individual users.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Right to Privacy was declared a fundamental right by the Supreme Court in the Puttaswamy judgment (2017).
Article 21 of the Indian Constitution deals with the protection of life and personal liberty.
Data protection law (DPDP Act 2023) is already in place, but new guidelines could refine its interpretation/implementation.
The judgment emphasizes informed consent and data minimization principles.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution of the right to privacy in India and its significance in the digital age.
Analyze the challenges in balancing national security, economic growth, and individual privacy rights.
Evaluate the adequacy of existing data protection frameworks in India and suggest improvements.
Examine the impact of such judicial pronouncements on government policies and corporate practices regarding data.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel resting on a stack of law books, with binary code flowing in the background.
GS2.trivia: The landmark K.S. Puttaswamy v. Union of India case in 2017 formally recognized the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right.

GS2.headline: India-ASEAN Summit Concludes: Pledges for Enhanced Trade, Digital Connectivity, and Maritime Security
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Summit concluded with leaders reaffirming their commitment to strengthening the Comprehensive Strategic Partnership.
• Key outcomes include agreements to boost bilateral trade to $200 billion by 2030 and enhance supply chain resilience.
• Discussions focused on expanding digital connectivity, promoting innovation, and cooperation in emerging technologies.
• Leaders also addressed regional security challenges, particularly in the Indo-Pacific, and emphasized maritime cooperation.
• India reiterated its 'Act East' policy and its vision for a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: ASEAN is a regional intergovernmental organization of ten Southeast Asian countries.
India's 'Act East' policy is a successor to the 'Look East' policy.
India and ASEAN elevated their relationship to a Comprehensive Strategic Partnership in 2022.
Key areas of cooperation include trade, connectivity, and maritime security.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of India-ASEAN relations for India's strategic and economic interests.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in deepening India's engagement with Southeast Asia.
Evaluate the role of India and ASEAN in maintaining stability and prosperity in the Indo-Pacific region.
Examine the impact of global geopolitical shifts on India's 'Act East' policy.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and ASEAN member states displayed together during a summit.
GS2.trivia: ASEAN was established on August 8, 1967, in Bangkok, Thailand, with five founding members: Indonesia, Malaysia, Philippines, Singapore, and Thailand.

GS2.headline: 'Poshan Abhiyaan' Progress Report: Significant Strides in Tackling Malnutrition, Challenges Remain
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development released its annual progress report on 'Poshan Abhiyaan' (National Nutrition Mission).
• The report highlighted significant reductions in stunting, wasting, and anemia among children and women across several states.
• Key successes were attributed to improved convergence of various schemes, community mobilization, and technological interventions like the Poshan Tracker.
• However, challenges persist, particularly in remote areas and among marginalized communities, requiring intensified efforts.
• The mission aims to achieve a malnutrition-free India by 2030, emphasizing a life-cycle approach to nutrition.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
GS2.source: Ministry of Women and Child Development
prelimsPoints: Poshan Abhiyaan (National Nutrition Mission) launched in 2018.
Aims to reduce stunting, wasting, anemia, and low birth weight.
Implemented by Ministry of Women and Child Development.
Utilizes technology like Poshan Tracker for monitoring.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-sectoral approach adopted by Poshan Abhiyaan to address malnutrition in India.
Analyze the socio-economic factors contributing to malnutrition and the challenges in its eradication.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government welfare schemes in improving nutritional outcomes.
Suggest policy interventions and community-level strategies to achieve a malnutrition-free India.
GS2.imageDescription: A graphic illustrating various components of Poshan Abhiyaan, with children and women at the center.
GS2.trivia: The word 'Poshan' means nutrition in Hindi. The mission was launched from Jhunjhunu, Rajasthan.

GS2.headline: Delimitation Commission for North-Eastern States: Progress and Challenges in Electoral Reorganization
GS2.explainer:
• The Delimitation Commission, tasked with redrawing Lok Sabha and Assembly constituencies in certain North-Eastern states, released its latest progress report.
• The exercise aims to ensure equitable representation based on population and geographical contiguity, as mandated by the Constitution.
• Challenges include addressing concerns of tribal communities, preserving administrative units, and managing local political sensitivities.
• The process is crucial for fair electoral representation and strengthening democratic institutions in the region.
• Public consultations are underway to gather feedback from various stakeholders before finalizing the proposals.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
prelimsPoints: Delimitation is the act of redrawing boundaries of Lok Sabha and state assembly constituencies.
Delimitation Commission is appointed by the President of India and works in collaboration with the Election Commission.
Its orders have the force of law and cannot be challenged in any court.
Last nationwide delimitation was completed in 2002, based on 2001 census data, but seats frozen until 2026.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional provisions and objectives behind the delimitation exercise in India.
Analyze the unique challenges faced by the Delimitation Commission in the North-Eastern states, particularly concerning tribal representation.
Examine the impact of delimitation on political representation, regional balance, and democratic fairness.
Evaluate the role of public consultations in ensuring transparency and addressing local concerns during the delimitation process.
GS2.imageDescription: A map of North-Eastern states with overlaid constituency boundaries.
GS2.trivia: The first Delimitation Commission was constituted in 1952. The next delimitation for the entire country is expected after 2026.

GS2.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Emphasizes Enhanced Cooperation in Indo-Pacific and Critical Technologies
GS2.explainer:
• The India-Japan Strategic Dialogue concluded with both nations reiterating their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
• Discussions focused on deepening defense and security cooperation, including joint exercises and technology transfers.
• Significant progress was made on collaboration in critical and emerging technologies, such as semiconductors, 5G, and artificial intelligence.
• Both sides also pledged to boost economic ties, infrastructure development, and clean energy projects.
• The dialogue underscored the importance of the Special Strategic and Global Partnership in addressing regional and global challenges.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India and Japan share a 'Special Strategic and Global Partnership'.
Cooperation extends to defense, economy, and critical technologies.
Both nations are part of the QUAD grouping.
Indo-Pacific vision is a key convergence area.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of India-Japan relations in the context of the evolving Indo-Pacific geopolitical landscape.
Discuss the various pillars of cooperation between India and Japan, including defense, economy, and technology.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in enhancing bilateral trade and investment between the two countries.
Evaluate the role of India-Japan partnership in promoting regional stability and economic prosperity.
GS2.imageDescription: National flags of India and Japan side-by-side, symbolizing bilateral relations.
GS2.trivia: Japan is one of the largest investors in India, particularly in infrastructure projects like the Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail.

GS2.headline: G20 Finance Ministers' Meeting Concludes: Focus on Global Economic Stability and Debt Sustainability
GS2.explainer:
• The G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors (FMCBG) meeting concluded with a joint communiqué addressing global economic challenges.
• Key discussions revolved around strengthening global financial safety nets, promoting debt sustainability for low-income countries, and coordinating macroeconomic policies.
• Ministers emphasized the need for structural reforms to boost long-term growth and resilience against future shocks.
• Climate finance and digital taxation frameworks were also high on the agenda, seeking equitable solutions.
• The meeting underscored the G20's role in fostering international economic cooperation amidst persistent geopolitical uncertainties.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: G20 is an international forum for governments and central bank governors from 19 countries and the European Union.
Focuses on global economic cooperation.
India held the G20 presidency in 2023.
Key agenda items often include financial stability, debt, and climate change.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the G20 forum in addressing contemporary global economic challenges.
Analyze the role of India in shaping the G20 agenda and promoting the interests of developing countries.
Examine the complexities of achieving global economic stability and debt sustainability in a fragmented world.
Evaluate the progress and challenges in international cooperation on climate finance and digital taxation within the G20 framework.
GS2.imageDescription: Official logo of the G20 with flags of member countries in the background.
GS2.trivia: The G20 was formed in 1999 in response to the Asian financial crisis as a forum for finance ministers and central bank governors. It was elevated to head of state/government level in 2008.

GS3.headline: National Green Hydrogen Mission: India Unveils Phase II for Accelerated Deployment
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet on National Sports Day approved the launch of Phase II of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, focusing on scaling up production and establishing export capabilities.
• This phase aims to incentivize manufacturing of electrolysers and develop green hydrogen hubs across key industrial corridors.
• Emphasis will be placed on R&D for cost reduction, skill development, and creating a robust regulatory framework.
• The mission seeks to position India as a global leader in green hydrogen production and utilization, contributing significantly to decarbonization targets.
• Public-private partnerships and international collaborations are key components of the strategy to attract investment and technology.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Investment models
Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation, environmental impact assessment
GS3.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission launched in 2023.
Phase II focuses on scaling up production and export capabilities.
Key components include electrolyser manufacturing incentives and green hydrogen hubs.
Aims for 5 MMT of green hydrogen production by 2030.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's energy security and climate change commitments through green hydrogen.
Analyze the economic potential and challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production.
Evaluate the role of government policies like PLI schemes in fostering a domestic green hydrogen ecosystem.
Examine the geopolitical implications of India becoming a green hydrogen exporter.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen production facility with solar panels and wind turbines in the background.
GS3.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all normal matter.

GS3.headline: RBI's Monetary Policy Review: Inflationary Pressures Keep Rates Steady Amidst Global Volatility
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) concluded its Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting, maintaining the repo rate at its current level.
• The decision reflects persistent concerns over inflation, primarily driven by volatile food prices and global commodity fluctuations.
• RBI reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the target while supporting economic growth.
• Experts anticipate a cautious approach by the central bank, closely monitoring both domestic and international economic indicators.
• Liquidity management measures were also discussed to ensure financial stability.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) sets the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India.
RBI's primary objective is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
CPI is the key inflation indicator in India.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth.
Discuss the effectiveness of various monetary policy tools in managing liquidity and inflation.
Examine the impact of global economic uncertainties on India's monetary policy decisions.
Evaluate the role of the MPC in ensuring transparency and accountability in monetary policy formulation.
GS3.imageDescription: RBI logo with graphs showing economic trends in the background.
GS3.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, in accordance with the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Critical Test of Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Landing Technology
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieved a significant milestone by successfully conducting a crucial test of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) landing technology.
• The test involved a scaled-down RLV prototype performing an autonomous landing on a designated runway, simulating conditions for future orbital re-entry.
• This demonstration is vital for developing India's capabilities in cost-effective space access and sustainable space exploration.
• The RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) project aims to develop all the necessary technologies for a Two Stage To Orbit (TSTO) fully reusable launch vehicle.
• Future phases will focus on orbital flight and re-entry experiments.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space, Computers, Robotics, Nano-technology, Bio-technology and issues relating to Intellectual Property Rights.
prelimsPoints: ISRO's RLV-TD project aims to develop reusable launch vehicle technology.
The test demonstrated autonomous landing capabilities.
Reusable launch vehicles reduce the cost of space missions.
India is among a few countries developing RLV technology.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of reusable launch vehicle technology for India's space program.
Analyze how RLV technology can make space access more affordable and sustainable.
Examine the potential applications and economic benefits of advanced space transportation systems.
Compare India's RLV efforts with those of other spacefaring nations.
GS3.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of ISRO's RLV-TD descending towards a runway.
GS3.trivia: The first RLV-TD 'HEX' (Hypersonic Flight Experiment) was successfully tested by ISRO in 2016.

GS3.headline: New Report Highlights Accelerated Melting of Himalayan Glaciers, Threatening Regional Water Security
GS3.explainer:
• A comprehensive report released by a consortium of environmental agencies revealed an alarming rate of melting of Himalayan glaciers.
• The study, based on satellite imagery and ground observations, indicates that glaciers are receding faster than previously estimated, posing a severe threat to water resources in South Asia.
• The accelerated melting is attributed to rising global temperatures and localized black carbon deposition.
• Consequences include increased flood risks in the short term and severe water scarcity for millions in the long term.
• The report calls for urgent international cooperation and stricter climate mitigation policies to protect this critical freshwater source.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
prelimsPoints: Himalayan glaciers are a critical source of water for major Asian rivers.
Glacier melting is accelerated by rising global temperatures and black carbon.
Impacts include increased flood risks and long-term water scarcity.
The Himalayas are often referred to as the 'Third Pole'.
mainsPoints: Analyze the multifaceted impacts of Himalayan glacier retreat on India and other South Asian countries.
Discuss the policy measures and international collaborations required to address the issue of climate change and glacier melting.
Examine the role of sustainable development practices and disaster management strategies in mitigating the consequences.
Evaluate the socio-economic implications of water scarcity due to glacier melt on agriculture and livelihoods.
GS3.imageDescription: A stark image of a receding Himalayan glacier with exposed rock and meltwater.
GS3.trivia: The Himalayas contain the third-largest deposit of ice and snow in the world, after the Arctic and Antarctic, earning them the nickname 'The Third Pole'.

GS3.headline: Government Accelerates Border Infrastructure Development to Enhance Connectivity and Vigilance
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence announced a significant push for upgrading border infrastructure, including roads, bridges, and advanced surveillance systems.
• The initiative aims to improve troop mobility, facilitate trade with neighboring countries, and enhance overall border security.
• Special focus is on strategically important regions along the northern and eastern borders, often characterized by challenging terrain.
• The project utilizes advanced engineering techniques and incorporates environmental sustainability measures.
• This development is crucial for national security and regional economic integration, bolstering India's defensive and logistical capabilities.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Border Roads Organization (BRO) is responsible for building and maintaining border roads.
Infrastructure development is crucial for troop movement and logistical support.
Focus on northern and eastern borders, including the LAC.
Enhances both defensive capabilities and regional trade.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of robust border infrastructure for India's national security.
Analyze the challenges involved in developing infrastructure in remote and difficult border areas.
Examine the role of border infrastructure in promoting regional trade and economic development.
Evaluate the impact of technological advancements in surveillance on border management.
GS3.imageDescription: A newly constructed bridge traversing a mountainous terrain in a border region.
GS3.trivia: The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) was formed in 1960 and has constructed over 60,000 km of roads in India's border areas.

GS3.headline: PLI Scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) Battery Manufacturing Gains Momentum
GS3.explainer:
• The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) battery manufacturing is showing robust progress, attracting significant investments.
• Several domestic and international players have committed to setting up large-scale battery manufacturing facilities in India.
• This initiative is crucial for India's push towards electric mobility and renewable energy storage, reducing reliance on imports.
• The scheme offers incentives based on sales of manufactured goods, promoting 'Make in India' and creating a domestic supply chain.
• It is expected to generate substantial employment and reduce the carbon footprint of the transportation and energy sectors.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: PLI scheme for ACC batteries aims to boost domestic manufacturing.
ACC batteries are critical for electric vehicles and renewable energy storage.
The scheme is administered by the Ministry of Heavy Industries.
It promotes 'Make in India' and reduces import dependence.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of PLI schemes in boosting India's manufacturing sector and achieving self-reliance.
Analyze the importance of ACC battery manufacturing for India's electric vehicle and renewable energy targets.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in establishing a robust domestic battery manufacturing ecosystem.
Evaluate the economic and environmental benefits of localizing ACC battery production.
GS3.imageDescription: An illustration of an electric car battery pack with the 'Make in India' logo.
GS3.trivia: The PLI scheme for ACC battery manufacturing has an outlay of ₹18,100 crore over five years.

GS3.headline: Wetlands Conservation Efforts Intensify: Focus on Ramsar Sites Expansion and Ecological Restoration
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change announced intensified efforts for wetlands conservation, including identifying new potential Ramsar Sites.
• The initiative aims to protect critical wetland ecosystems that provide vital services like water purification, flood control, and biodiversity support.
• Emphasis is placed on community participation, sustainable management practices, and ecological restoration of degraded wetlands.
• India, a signatory to the Ramsar Convention, has significantly expanded its network of 'Wetlands of International Importance' in recent years.
• This push aligns with India's commitments to international biodiversity conventions and sustainable development goals.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and its conservation.
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Ramsar Convention is an international treaty for the conservation and sustainable use of wetlands.
Ramsar Sites are wetlands designated to be of international importance.
India has a significant number of Ramsar Sites (currently 80, but could increase by 2025).
Wetlands provide crucial ecosystem services like water regulation and biodiversity support.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological and economic importance of wetlands and the threats they face in India.
Analyze the role of the Ramsar Convention and national policies in wetlands conservation.
Examine the challenges in managing and restoring wetlands, including encroachment and pollution.
Suggest strategies for effective community-based wetland management and sustainable utilization.
GS3.imageDescription: A serene wetland landscape with migratory birds and lush vegetation.
GS3.trivia: Chilika Lake in Odisha was the first Indian wetland designated as a Ramsar Site in 1981.

GS3.headline: India's First Dedicated Quantum Computing Lab Inaugurated, Boosting Research and Innovation
GS3.explainer:
• India's first dedicated Quantum Computing Lab was officially inaugurated, marking a significant leap in the country's technological capabilities.
• The state-of-the-art facility will serve as a hub for advanced research, development, and training in quantum technologies.
• It aims to foster collaboration between academia, industry, and government agencies to accelerate India's position in the global quantum race.
• Initial focus areas include quantum algorithms, quantum cryptography, and quantum sensing, with applications in defense, finance, and healthcare.
• This initiative is a crucial step under the National Quantum Mission, positioning India as a key player in future computing paradigms.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space, Computers, Robotics, Nano-technology, Bio-technology and issues relating to Intellectual Property Rights.
prelimsPoints: Quantum computing utilizes principles of quantum mechanics.
India launched the National Quantum Mission in 2023.
Qubits are the basic units of information in quantum computing.
Applications include drug discovery, cryptography, and AI.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of quantum computing and its strategic importance for India.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in developing a robust quantum technology ecosystem in India.
Examine the ethical and security implications of quantum computing advancements.
Evaluate the role of government missions and private sector investment in accelerating quantum research and development.
GS3.imageDescription: An abstract representation of quantum computing, with glowing circuits and intertwined particles.
GS3.trivia: Unlike classical computers that use bits (0 or 1), quantum computers use 'qubits' which can represent 0, 1, or both simultaneously through superposition.

GS3.headline: PM Jan Dhan Yojana Marks 11th Anniversary: Financial Inclusion Reaches New Milestones
GS3.explainer:
• The Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana (PMJDY) completed 11 successful years since its launch, celebrating significant achievements in financial inclusion.
• The scheme has enabled over 50 crore bank accounts, bringing a vast unbanked population into the formal financial system.
• PMJDY accounts have been instrumental in direct benefit transfers (DBT) of various government welfare schemes.
• The initiative has also boosted financial literacy, access to credit, insurance, and pension facilities, especially for women and rural populations.
• Future focus includes deepening digital transactions and promoting micro-insurance products through the Jan Dhan ecosystem.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
prelimsPoints: PMJDY was launched on August 28, 2014, for financial inclusion.
Provides basic savings bank accounts, RuPay debit cards, and overdraft facilities.
Key for Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) of government welfare schemes.
Administered by the Ministry of Finance.
mainsPoints: Discuss the impact of PM Jan Dhan Yojana on financial inclusion and poverty reduction in India.
Analyze how PMJDY has facilitated the effective delivery of government welfare schemes through DBT.
Examine the challenges in achieving complete financial literacy and digital penetration among PMJDY account holders.
Evaluate the future potential of the Jan Dhan ecosystem in driving inclusive growth and economic empowerment.
GS3.imageDescription: A stylized graphic showing the PMJDY logo with hands holding a RuPay card and mobile phone.
GS3.trivia: PMJDY accounts come with a RuPay Debit Card, which also provides accidental insurance cover.

sources: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports
Ministry of Women and Child Development
Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
